cues.narration: Welcome to ArtsFolio Training Video 04: Events and Public History. In this video, we’ll create and edit event records, choose exact or month-and-year dates, add locations and live links, and review the complete event history.
Click Events in the sidebar. This page is the working history of exhibitions, talks, fairs, installations, residencies, and other public activity.
Review the existing records before adding another. Consistent titles, dates, and venue names keep the public history from becoming a thicket.
Events are presented according to their date information and the ordering supported by the page. Accurate dates are therefore part of the design, not merely clerical detail.
Choose the control for adding a new event. Begin with a concise title that a visitor will recognize.
ArtsFolio stores the public event date as flexible text. You can enter a complete date range, a single date, or only a month and year when that is the most accurate historical record.
Write the date exactly as you want visitors to read it. Examples include October 8, 2026, June through September 2026, or simply March 2024. Do not invent a day when only the month is known.
Add the venue and location information a visitor needs. Avoid private shorthand that only makes sense inside your studio.
Use event type to identify the activity, such as a solo exhibition, group exhibition, artist talk, residency, or collection milestone. The work field can name artwork associated with the event.
Use the description for useful public context. Keep private logistics, internal notes, and personal contact information out of this field.
Save when the essential information is complete, and read any validation message before leaving the page.
Open an existing event to review its complete record.
Check the title for clarity and consistency with exhibition materials or institutional listings.
Review the public date text carefully. Keep formatting consistent across the history while preserving uncertainty when the exact day is not known.
Confirm venue and location names. Consistent naming prevents one institution from appearing as several unrelated places.
Confirm the event type and any associated work names. Consistent language makes the public history easier to scan and understand.
Read the description as a visitor would. It should explain the event without sounding like a database export wearing a necktie.
Save the edited event and confirm that ArtsFolio reports success.
Return to the Events list after saving. Review the records together as an exhibition history rather than as isolated entries.
Check the order of the records and make sure dates are formatted consistently. Important exhibitions and milestones should be easy to find.
Review the status controls for each event. Use the available status carefully so incomplete or internal records are not treated as finished.
Scan venue names, locations, event types, and associated work names for duplicates or spelling variations. Consistency keeps the history readable and credible.
A reliable workflow is record the event, verify the public date text, save it, return to the list, and review its place in the complete history.

You now know how to create and edit events, use exact or month-and-year dates, manage locations and live links, and review your exhibition history. Next, we’ll review messages, contact submissions, and email signups.
